rows.title: Hi, Monkey!
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the monkey! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the monkey back.
"glorp?" whispered Cole. "glorp?" whispered the monkey. The cheese tipped over by itself.
"glorp?" said Cole. "glorp!" said the monkey. Then the cheese wiggled. Cole hopped one more time, just to make a point. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly silver.
Cole waved at the monkey. "Bye for now!" said Cole. The monkey waved back.

rows.title: Cole and the Pasta
paragraphs: "Come on, duck!" said Cole. The duck came. Cole led the way.
"rubber duck!" said Cole. "rubber duck!" said the duck. The pasta hopped. Once.
Cole sang "rubber duck." The duck sang it too. The pasta joined in. Cole swayed without thinking. It was a thing they did now. There was a purple feeling to the moment that nobody really named.
Cole picked up the duck for a hug. "Night, duck," said Cole. Then Cole curled up.

rows.title: The Comet and the Pig
paragraphs: Cole spotted the pig from across the room. Cole waved. The pig waved back.
"gloop!" said Cole. "gloop!" said the pig. Then the comet sneezed.
"gloop!" said Cole. "gloop!" said the pig. The comet hopped. Once. A short burst of ran happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little apple red.
Now Cole is sleepy. The pig is sleepy too. Good night, pig. Good night, Cole.

rows.title: Cole Sees the Helicopter
paragraphs: Cole grabbed the pony's paw. "Ready?" said Cole. The pony nodded.
"glorp?" whispered Cole. "glorp?" whispered the pony. The helicopter tipped over by itself.
Cole sang "glorp." The pony sang it too. The helicopter joined in. Cole peeked one more time, just to make a point. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little white.
Cole picked up the pony for a hug. "Night, pony," said Cole. Then Cole curled up.

rows.title: Cole and the Fish
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the fish! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the fish back.
Cole sang "splat." The fish sang it too. The cookie joined in.
"splat!" said Cole. "splat!" said the fish. Then the cookie sneezed. Cole peeked briefly, then pretended that had not happened. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly apple red.
Cole waved at the fish. "Bye for now!" said Cole. The fish waved back.

rows.title: Cole and the Moon
paragraphs: Cole grabbed the bunny's paw. "Ready?" said Cole. The bunny nodded.
"tiny piano!" said Cole. "tiny piano!" said the bunny. Then the moon sneezed.
"tiny piano!" said Cole. "tiny piano!" said the bunny. The moon hopped. Once. Cole wiggled without thinking. It was a thing they did now. A faint apple red glow hung over the scene by then.
Time for a hug. Cole hugged the bunny. Then Cole yawned. The bunny yawned too.

rows.title: The Rainbow and the Fish
paragraphs: Cole grabbed the fish's paw. "Ready?" said Cole. The fish nodded.
"glorp?" whispered Cole. "glorp?" whispered the fish. The rainbow tipped over by itself.
"glorp!" said Cole. "glorp!" said the fish. The rainbow hopped. Once. Halfway through, Cole wiggled for absolutely no reason. There was a orange feeling to the moment that nobody really named.
Cole hugged the fish one more time. "Come back tomorrow?" said Cole. The fish nodded.

rows.title: Cole and the Peas
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the turtle! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the turtle back.
"bonk!" said Cole. "bonk!" said the turtle. Then the peas sneezed.
"bonk?" said Cole. "bonk!" said the turtle. Then the peas wiggled. There was a small jumped moment that nobody quite witnessed in full. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little rainbow.
Cole waved at the turtle. "Bye for now!" said Cole. The turtle waved back.

rows.title: Cole and the Bubbles
paragraphs: Cole ran outside. The chick was right there. "Hi chick!" said Cole.
"paper crown!" said Cole. "paper crown!" said the chick. The bubbles hopped. Once.

"paper crown!" said Cole. "paper crown!" said the chick. Then the bubbles sneezed. There was a small clapped moment that nobody quite witnessed in full. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly white.
Cole waved at the chick. "Bye for now!" said Cole. The chick waved back.

rows.title: Cole Says Hi!
paragraphs: Cole opened the door. There was the cat! "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi!" said the cat back.
Cole sang "paper crown." The cat sang it too. The pancakes joined in.
"paper crown!" said Cole. "paper crown!" said the cat. Then the pancakes sneezed. Cole marched without thinking. It was a thing they did now. The apple red thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Cole hugged the cat one more time. "Come back tomorrow?" said Cole. The cat nodded.

rows.title: The Apple Slices Day
paragraphs: It was a bright morning. Cole grabbed the pony and headed out. "We have a plan," said Cole.
"flumpy!" said Cole. The pony said it back. Then they said it together. The apple slices hummed along, kind of.

"flumpy," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The apple slices flipped over by itself. The pony looked impressed. A short burst of floated happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details. The light shifted briefly toward striped and then thought better of it.
Cole pulled the pony into a small pillow fort. "We made it," said Cole. The pony yawned.

rows.title: Cole and the Rubber Chicken
paragraphs: Cole spotted the bear cub across the yard. "Hey bear cub!" said Cole. The bear cub was already smiling.
"boing," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The rubber chicken flipped over by itself. The bear cub looked impressed.

"boing!" said Cole. The bear cub said it back. Then they said it together. The rubber chicken hummed along, kind of. A short burst of twirled happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details. The golden thing was happening again, whatever it was.
By the end of the day, Cole and the bear cub were happy and ready for whatever came next. They high-fived and headed home.

rows.title: How Cole Found the Wobbly Telescope
paragraphs: Cole packed a tiny bag and called the seal. The seal came running. Cole led the way.
"sneezy sandwich!" said Cole. The seal said it back. Then they said it together. The wobbly telescope hummed along, kind of.

"sneezy sandwich," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The wobbly telescope flipped over by itself. The seal looked impressed. Cole skipped without thinking. It was a thing they did now. Cole noticed a streak of orange across the wall. Or did they.
Cole grabbed the seal's paw. "Onto the next thing," said Cole. They walked home together.

rows.title: The Cookies Day
paragraphs: Cole told the foal, "Today is going to be a big one." The foal agreed. They were ready.
"kabloom!" said Cole. The foal said it back. Then they said it together. The cookies hummed along, kind of.

"kabloom," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The cookies flipped over by itself. The foal looked impressed. Cole flopped without thinking. It was a thing they did now. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly pink.
Cole tucked the foal in first, then climbed in too. "Same time tomorrow?" said Cole. The foal was already half asleep.

rows.title: Cole and the Hedgehog
paragraphs: Cole packed a tiny bag and called the hedgehog. The hedgehog came running. Cole led the way.
"sneezy-pants," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The cookies flipped over by itself. The hedgehog looked impressed.

Cole tapped the cookies like a button. "sneezy-pants!" said Cole. "sneezy-pants!" said the hedgehog. The cookies did exactly nothing, which was funny. Cole galloped without thinking. It was a thing they did now. The light shifted briefly toward sandy tan and then thought better of it.
By the end of the day, Cole and the hedgehog were tired and happy. They hugged. Then they went to bed.

rows.title: The Clipboard Adventure
paragraphs: Cole spotted the lamb across the yard. "Hey lamb!" said Cole. The lamb was already smiling.
Cole tapped the clipboard like a button. "blorp!" said Cole. "blorp!" said the lamb. The clipboard did exactly nothing, which was funny.

"blorp!" said Cole. The lamb said it back. Then they said it together. The clipboard hummed along, kind of. Cole floated briefly, then pretended that had not happened. Cole noticed a streak of crimson across the wall. Or did they.
Cole grabbed the lamb's paw. "Onto the next thing," said Cole. They walked home together.

rows.title: Cole and the Pancakes
paragraphs: Cole told the koala, "Today is going to be a big one." The koala agreed. They were ready.
"kabloom!" said Cole. The koala said it back. Then they said it together. The pancakes hummed along, kind of.

Cole tapped the pancakes like a button. "kabloom!" said Cole. "kabloom!" said the koala. The pancakes did exactly nothing, which was funny. Cole waved one more time, just to make a point. The sandy tan thing was happening again, whatever it was.
By the end of the day, Cole and the koala were happy and ready for whatever came next. They high-fived and headed home.

rows.title: How Cole Found the Apology Balloon
paragraphs: Cole spotted the piglet across the yard. "Hey piglet!" said Cole. The piglet was already smiling.
"jiggly blorp!" said Cole. The piglet said it back. Then they said it together. The apology balloon hummed along, kind of.

"jiggly blorp," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The apology balloon flipped over by itself. The piglet looked impressed. Somewhere in there, Cole bounced for emphasis. There was a golden feeling to the moment that nobody really named.
By the end of the day, Cole and the piglet were happy and ready for whatever came next. They high-fived and headed home.

rows.title: Cole and the Strawberries
paragraphs: Cole spotted the puppy across the yard. "Hey puppy!" said Cole. The puppy was already smiling.
"flumpy!" said Cole. The puppy said it back. Then they said it together. The strawberries hummed along, kind of.

"flumpy," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The strawberries flipped over by itself. The puppy looked impressed. For exactly two seconds, Cole skidded like it was a job. The silver thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Cole grabbed the puppy's paw. "Onto the next thing," said Cole. They walked home together.

rows.title: Cole and the Hedgehog
paragraphs: Cole opened a door and the hedgehog tumbled in. "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi back!" said the hedgehog — somehow.
"fwoosh!" said Cole. The hedgehog said it back. Then they said it together. The dramatic cape hummed along, kind of.

"fwoosh," whispered Cole, like it was a spell. The dramatic cape flipped over by itself. The hedgehog looked impressed. Cole floated without thinking. It was a thing they did now. Everything in the room had picked up a faint rainbow tint.
Cole tucked the hedgehog in first, then climbed in too. "Same time tomorrow?" said Cole. The hedgehog was already half asleep.

rows.title: Cole and the Crow Take the Stage
paragraphs: The whole thing rested on one sticker sheet. Cole had practiced. The crow had practiced. The forest path was, technically, ready.
The crow blanked. The sticker sheet hit the floor a second later. Two crises at once.

Cole skated and shouted "mister floof!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. Everything in the room had picked up a faint golden tint. There was a sleepy quality to the air, if anyone noticed. Somebody had brought spaghetti. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.

"mister floof!" yelled Cole at the broken sticker sheet. The sticker sheet twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. A dinosaur watched the whole thing happen and offered no comment.
Curtain down. Cole high-fived the crow. "bonkledink?" said Cole. "bonkledink!" said the crow. The new catchphrase had landed.

rows.title: Who Took the Grilled Cheese?
paragraphs: Cole had carried the grilled cheese all the way over to the school bus line. The parrot tagged along. The whole morning had been building to this.
The grilled cheese had vanished. Cole felt spooky and unusually focused about it. The mermaid noticed and tried to act normal. It was not working.
Plot twist nobody saw coming except maybe the parrot: it was the parrot. The parrot had been the grilled cheese thief the whole time. It was very sorry.

Cole looked straight at the mermaid and said one word: "crusty pretzel." The mermaid confessed immediately. To a different crime. Cole noted it for later. A short burst of charged happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little tomato red.
Bedtime came. Cole yawned. The parrot yawned too. Just before sleep, the parrot mumbled "noodle scarf" into the pillow, like it was the official sign-off now.

rows.title: The Day Cole Tried to Hide So Well That Nobody Finds Them
paragraphs: Cole woke up with a plan. Today, at the taco stand, Cole was going to hide so well that nobody finds them. The eagle was in. The eagle was always in.
The fairy stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt clumsy about it. The fairy did not look like it was going to move on its own.

Cole walked up to the fairy in full clumsy mode. Not asking permission. Not apologizing. Just clumsy. The fairy had not prepared for clumsy. The fairy took a small step back. And one of them, very quietly, said "magnet shoe." Somebody had brought fried chicken. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.

Cole said "bonk" once, calmly. The fairy suddenly remembered somewhere else it needed to be. Cole hid so well that nobody could find them through the gap. The eagle noted this for later. Cole skated briefly, then pretended that had not happened. There was a sunset orange feeling to the moment that nobody really named.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The eagle kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: Cole and the Koala Take the Stage
paragraphs: Show day. Cole had a backpack zipper, a co-star (koala), and a stage (treehouse). The plan was rock solid. Or rock-ish.
The koala blanked. The backpack zipper hit the floor a second later. Two crises at once.
Cole improvised. "damp toast!" Then charged. Then "damp toast!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT. Off to the side, some waffles sat there, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.

Cole pointed at the broken backpack zipper: "damp toast." The backpack zipper reassembled itself in roughly the wrong order. The koala applauded the wrong order. The show was now better. The midnight blue thing was happening again, whatever it was. A "snorble-doo" hung in the air for a second longer than expected. Underneath everything, Cole was running on pure brave. Off to the side, the substitute teacher was processing all of this with visible difficulty.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the koala. The show was unrepeatable, which felt right. Time to pack up and find the next thing.

rows.title: Cole and the Raccoon Take the Stage
paragraphs: Show day. Cole had a shopping list, a co-star (raccoon), and a stage (moon base). The plan was rock solid. Or rock-ish.
The raccoon blanked. The shopping list hit the floor a second later. Two crises at once.

Cole improvised. "moon spoon!" Then danced. Then "moon spoon!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT. Everything in the room had picked up a faint neon tint. The whole day had a sleepy energy to it. Nobody could explain why. Meanwhile, some waffles waited patiently for its moment. Off to the side, the tiny wizard was processing all of this with visible difficulty.

"moon spoon!" yelled Cole at the broken shopping list. The shopping list twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. And one of them, very quietly, said "jiggly blorp."
Backstage, Cole and the raccoon looked at each other and at the exact same moment said "moon spoon." That was the show's name now, apparently. Forever.

rows.title: The Wind-up Toy Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: Backstage at the maze, Cole gave the wind-up toy one final inspection. The hamster was already in character. Showtime in thirty.
The wind-up toy launched itself, bounced once, and landed in the hamster's lap. The look on the hamster's face was unsurvivable.

Cole improvised. "booger cloud!" Then marched. Then "booger cloud!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT. The whole day had a spooky energy to it. Nobody could explain why. Off to the side, the substitute teacher was processing all of this with visible difficulty.

"booger cloud!" yelled Cole at the broken wind-up toy. The wind-up toy twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Cole noticed a streak of burnt orange across the wall. Or did they. Just then a "wubba" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it. Off to the side, a piece of sushi sat there, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the hamster. The show was unrepeatable, which felt right. Time to pack up and find the next thing.

rows.title: Cole's Big Show
paragraphs: Show day. Cole had a lunch tray, a co-star (goldfish), and a stage (cafeteria). The plan was rock solid. Or rock-ish.
The lunch tray launched itself, bounced once, and landed in the goldfish's lap. The look on the goldfish's face was unsurvivable.

Cole danced and shouted "spoon hat!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. Cole noticed a streak of burnt orange across the wall. Or did they. There was a silly quality to the air, if anyone noticed. Meanwhile, a grilled cheese waited patiently for its moment. A sock monster watched the whole thing happen and offered no comment.
"spoon hat!" yelled Cole at the broken lunch tray. The lunch tray twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it.
Curtain down. Cole high-fived the goldfish. "jiggly blorp?" said Cole. "jiggly blorp!" said the goldfish. The new catchphrase had landed.

rows.title: Cole and the Fennec Fox Try to Finish the Giant Snack Stack
paragraphs: It started at the upside-down museum. Cole looked at the fennec fox and made up their mind: today they would finish the giant snack stack. No matter what.
The robot stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt dramatic about it. The robot did not look like it was going to move on its own.

And then Cole did the most silver thing imaginable: walked right past the robot while pretending to be a silver traffic cone. The robot watched, confused. The plan was working. A short burst of galloped happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details.

Cole said "Captain Noodle" once, calmly. The robot suddenly remembered somewhere else it needed to be. Cole finished the giant snack stack through the gap. The fennec fox noted this for later. Just then a "sneezy-pants" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it. Off to the side, some pretzels sat there, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The fennec fox kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: Cole vs the Lunch Wizard
paragraphs: Cole pulled the wolf aside at the dog park. The plan was simple: collect seven shiny things. The wolf nodded immediately. It was on.
There was just one problem: a lunch wizard stood between Cole and the goal. "Nope," said the lunch wizard. "Not today. Not collect seven shiny things. Not on my watch."

Cole walked up to the lunch wizard in full sneaky mode. Not asking permission. Not apologizing. Just sneaky. The lunch wizard had not prepared for sneaky. The lunch wizard took a small step back. For exactly two seconds, Cole galloped like it was a job. There was a pitch black feeling to the moment that nobody really named.

Cole said "bubble wand" once, calmly. The lunch wizard suddenly remembered somewhere else it needed to be. Cole collected the seven shiny things through the gap. The wolf noted this for later. Later someone would swear they heard "wobble-flop" in the rafters. Meanwhile, some cereal waited patiently for its moment.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The wolf kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: Cole and the fennec fox had arrived at the grocery aisle with a simple plan: have a regular afternoon. The fennec fox was good at regular afternoons.
A wall of "no" landed between Cole and the donuts. Cole went very clumsy. The talking sandwich sensed escalation.
Cole tumbled sideways while holding the water bottle. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back.

"flumpy!" said Cole. The fennec fox nodded. Three witnesses appeared from nowhere. They also nodded. The talking sandwich tried to recount. The recount confirmed the loophole. Everything in the room had picked up a faint sunset orange tint. Just then a "soggy sock" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.
In bed, Cole thought about the loophole and quietly said "flumpy" one more time, just to seal it. The fennec fox said "flumpy" too, like a co-signature. Bedtime: earned.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the spectacularly average hallway. Cole and the melodramatic cat were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.
Off-limits, said the sparkly squid, pointing at the aggressively normal toast. Cole turned a particular shade of suspiciously prepared. The sparkly squid had not seen that energy before.
Cole tripped magnificently sideways while holding the shopping list. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back.

The sparkly squid tried to amend the rule on the fly. The sparkly squid said, "Actually—" Cole held the shopping list higher. The sparkly squid could not actually argue against that. The amendment was abandoned. Cole noticed a streak of deep crimson across the wall. Or did they. A distant "fluffypants" echoed from somewhere, possibly a memory.
Cole held the aggressively normal toast up like a trophy. The sparkly squid could only watch. "squonk!" yelled Cole. Both things were now true at once.
The melodramatic cat watched Cole yawn and yawned too. "squonk," said Cole, using it for the last time today. The melodramatic cat approved. Sleep.

rows.title: The Day the Haunted Tea Vanished
paragraphs: There was the moon base. There was Cole. There was the philosophical owl. And there, briefly, was the haunted tea.
No haunted tea anywhere. Cole went suspiciously cheerful like a tiny detective. The philosophical crab hummed a song nobody had asked for.

Cole put on their most suspiciously cheerful expression and walked very slowly toward the philosophical crab. The philosophical crab tried to back away. "Tell me what you saw," said Cole, staying suspiciously cheerful. The philosophical crab cracked immediately. The obviously red thing was happening again, whatever it was. A "pickle wizard" hung in the air for a second longer than expected.

The trail led to the philosophical owl. Of course. The philosophical owl had a single crumb of haunted tea on its face. "YOU?" said Cole. The philosophical owl looked away politely. Cole sprinted incorrectly briefly, then pretended that had not happened.
Cole looked straight at the philosophical crab and said one word: "soggy sock." The philosophical crab confessed immediately. To a different crime. Cole noted it for later.
Back home, Cole high-fived the philosophical owl over the case being officially closed. They both started planning the next caper. Justice, snacks, then more justice.

rows.title: Cole vs the Courtroom Duck
paragraphs: Cole had carried the aggressively normal toast all the way over to the ferry. The exasperated flamingo tagged along. The whole morning had been building to this.
The aggressively normal toast had vanished. Cole turned quietly heroic in a way the courtroom duck could feel from across the room. The courtroom duck coughed for no reason.

Cole put on their most quietly heroic expression and walked very slowly toward the courtroom duck. The courtroom duck tried to back away. "Tell me what you saw," said Cole, staying quietly heroic. The courtroom duck cracked immediately.

Then the exasperated flamingo burped. A specific burp. The kind of burp that smells exactly like aggressively normal toast. "Oh," said Cole. The exasperated flamingo tried to look surprised. It was not convincing. Cole meandered thoughtfully one more time, just to make a point. "sneezy sandwich," went somebody. Nobody asked who.

Cole looked straight at the courtroom duck and said one word: "sproing." The courtroom duck confessed immediately. To a different crime. Cole noted it for later. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little deep crimson.
Back home, Cole high-fived the exasperated flamingo over the case being officially closed. They both started planning the next caper. Justice, snacks, then more justice.

rows.title: Cole and the Melodramatic Cat Take the Stage
paragraphs: The whole thing rested on one noisy spoon. Cole had practiced. The melodramatic cat had practiced. The pony field was, technically, ready.
The noisy spoon snapped. Right in the middle. Cole and the melodramatic cat stared at each half.
Cole improvised. "wibble-whap!" Then flailed politely. Then "wibble-whap!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT. Everything in the room had picked up a faint impossible green tint.
The sparkly squid heckled, genuinely. The melodramatic cat hissed. Cole kept going. The conflict made the bit. Somebody had brought overconfident pudding. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.

"wibble-whap!" yelled Cole at the broken noisy spoon. The noisy spoon twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. You could call the mood gloriously incorrect, and nobody would disagree.
Curtain down. Cole high-fived the melodramatic cat. "booger cloud?" said Cole. "booger cloud!" said the melodramatic cat. The new catchphrase had landed.

rows.title: The Mascot Head Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: The whole thing rested on one mascot head. Cole had practiced. The suspicious duck had practiced. The cafeteria was, technically, ready.
The mascot head launched itself, bounced once, and landed in the suspicious duck's lap. The look on the suspicious duck's face was unsurvivable.
Cole improvised. "slime puddle!" Then waltzed accidentally. Then "slime puddle!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT. Cole kept feeling accidentally important about the whole thing.
The retired wizard tried to leave. The suspicious duck blocked the exit, on purpose, somehow. Cole now had a captive audience.

"slime puddle!" yelled Cole at the broken mascot head. The mascot head twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Cole noticed a streak of wildly pleasant blue across the wall. Or did they. Somebody had brought emergency burrito. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
Curtain down. Cole high-fived the suspicious duck. "gloop?" said Cole. "gloop!" said the suspicious duck. The new catchphrase had landed.

rows.title: Who Took the Aggressively Normal Toast?
paragraphs: Cole had carried the aggressively normal toast all the way over to the nurse's office. The tiny horse tagged along. The whole morning had been building to this.
The aggressively normal toast had vanished. Cole got formally ridiculous about it. The melodramatic ghost tried a slow shrug. The shrug arrived late and crooked.

Cole put on their most formally ridiculous expression and walked very slowly toward the melodramatic ghost. The melodramatic ghost tried to back away. "Tell me what you saw," said Cole, staying formally ridiculous. The melodramatic ghost cracked immediately.

The trail led to the tiny horse. Of course. The tiny horse had a single crumb of aggressively normal toast on its face. "YOU?" said Cole. The tiny horse looked away politely. Cole stared bravely one more time, just to make a point.

Cole looked straight at the melodramatic ghost and said one word: "banana phone." The melodramatic ghost confessed immediately. To a different crime. Cole noted it for later. The eerie periwinkle thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Bedtime came. Cole yawned. The tiny horse yawned too. Just before sleep, the tiny horse mumbled "burp bubble" into the pillow, like it was the official sign-off now.

rows.title: The Day Cole Saved the Show
paragraphs: Show day. Cole had a dramatic cape, a co-star (philosophical owl), and a stage (underground ballroom). The plan was rock solid. Or rock-ish.
The dramatic cape refused. Just refused. The philosophical owl poked it twice. Nothing. Cole had to come up with something.
Cole improvised. "snorble-doo!" Then hovered suspiciously. Then "snorble-doo!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT. Underneath everything, Cole was running on pure thoughtfully menacing.

A bewildered sphinx in the audience laughed too loudly. The philosophical owl mistook it for applause and bowed. Cole rolled with it. Different show now. A "soggy genius" hung in the air for a second longer than expected. Meanwhile, a slab of extremely bold lasagna waited patiently for its moment.

Cole pointed at the broken dramatic cape: "snorble-doo." The dramatic cape reassembled itself in roughly the wrong order. The philosophical owl applauded the wrong order. The show was now better. The shimmering gold thing was happening again, whatever it was.
Backstage, Cole and the philosophical owl looked at each other and at the exact same moment said "snorble-doo." That was the show's name now, apparently. Forever.

rows.title: The Library Card Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: The whole thing rested on one library card. Cole had practiced. The tiny alpaca had practiced. The hallway was, technically, ready.
The tiny alpaca blanked. The library card hit the floor a second later. Two crises at once.

Out came the only word that fit: "Captain Noodle." Cole skipped solemnly for emphasis. The bit was a different bit now. Cole noticed a streak of electric violet across the wall. Or did they. Later someone would swear they heard "wubba" in the rafters. Underneath everything, Cole was running on pure surprisingly enthusiastic.
The substitute principal heckled, genuinely. The tiny alpaca hissed. Cole kept going. The conflict made the bit.

Cole pointed at the broken library card: "Captain Noodle." The library card reassembled itself in roughly the wrong order. The tiny alpaca applauded the wrong order. The show was now better. Somebody had brought haunted tea. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the tiny alpaca. The show was unrepeatable, which felt right. Time to pack up and find the next thing.

rows.title: Cole and the Overly Formal Otter Take the Stage
paragraphs: Show day. Cole had a invisible box, a co-star (overly formal otter), and a stage (blanket fort). The plan was rock solid. Or rock-ish.
The invisible box launched itself, bounced once, and landed in the overly formal otter's lap. The look on the overly formal otter's face was unsurvivable.

Out came the only word that fit: "pickle juice." Cole tripped magnificently for emphasis. The bit was a different bit now. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly forgotten gray. Cole kept feeling professionally confused about the whole thing.
A indignant mushroom in the audience laughed too loudly. The overly formal otter mistook it for applause and bowed. Cole rolled with it. Different show now.

Cole pointed at the broken invisible box: "pickle juice." The invisible box reassembled itself in roughly the wrong order. The overly formal otter applauded the wrong order. The show was now better. Somebody had brought forbidden waffles. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
Curtain down. Cole high-fived the overly formal otter. "jiggly blorp?" said Cole. "jiggly blorp!" said the overly formal otter. The new catchphrase had landed.

rows.title: Cole and the Emergency Noodles Mystery
paragraphs: Cole had carried the emergency noodles all the way over to the park. The mischievous fox tagged along. The whole morning had been building to this.
The emergency noodles had vanished. Cole turned gravely unimpressed in a way the confused dragon could feel from across the room. The confused dragon coughed for no reason.
Cole spotted it: a tiny deep crimson crumb on the floor. Then another. Then another. The crumbs were leading somewhere. They were not leading to the confused dragon.

The mischievous fox suddenly found something fascinating on the ceiling. "Hey," said Cole. The mischievous fox would not look down. The emergency noodles had vanished. So had the mischievous fox's alibi. Connected.

Cole looked straight at the confused dragon and said one word: "mister floof." The confused dragon confessed immediately. To a different crime. Cole noted it for later. For exactly two seconds, Cole scuttled with purpose like it was a job. "crinkle-bonk," went somebody. Nobody asked who.
Back home, Cole high-fived the mischievous fox over the case being officially closed. They both started planning the next caper. Justice, snacks, then more justice.

rows.title: Cole and the Pigeon Try to Plan a Surprise Party
paragraphs: Today's mission, agreed silently between Cole and the pigeon at the garage: plan a surprise party. Cole did not love announcing missions out loud, but this one felt different.
The hallway monitor stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt weirdly invested about it. The hallway monitor did not look like it was going to move on its own.
Cole chaotically bolted like a professional who had committed to a specific kind of unhinged. The hallway monitor had not budgeted for this. Cole did not break eye contact.

The hallway monitor tried something with the boba tea. Cole mentally screenshotted it. Could be evidence later. Probably wouldn't be. The light shifted briefly toward acid yellow and then thought better of it. "honk," went somebody. Nobody asked who.
Cole said "noodle scarf" once, calmly. The hallway monitor suddenly remembered somewhere else it needed to be. Cole planned the surprise party through the gap. The pigeon noted this for later.
In bed that night, Cole catalogued the day. The pigeon was already asleep, technically a co-conspirator. "noodle scarf," said Cole, one last time. Quietly, but on the record.

rows.title: Cole and the Panda Try to Win the Silly Race
paragraphs: One objective today, at the pet store: win the silly race. The panda knew it. Cole knew it. Everybody else would find out shortly.
The substitute coach stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt weirdly proud about it. The substitute coach did not look like it was going to move on its own.

Cole passive-aggressively waved like a professional who had committed to a specific kind of unhinged. The substitute coach had not budgeted for this. Cole did not break eye contact. The light shifted briefly toward burnt orange and then thought better of it.
The substitute coach tried something with the cold pizza. Cole mentally screenshotted it. Could be evidence later. Probably wouldn't be.
Cole said "cheese hat" once, calmly. The substitute coach suddenly remembered somewhere else it needed to be. Cole won the silly race through the gap. The panda noted this for later.
On the way home, Cole played it back. The panda kept bouncing along. "flumpy," said Cole, filing it. The panda heard and stamped its approval, which is a thing the panda can apparently do now.

rows.title: How Cole Outsmarted the Cryptid
paragraphs: Over by the upside-down museum, Cole was doing the bare minimum. So was the koala. Suspiciously calm energy. Things were technically fine. They would not stay fine.
Off-limits, said the cryptid, pointing at the vending machine pretzels. Cole turned a particular shade of minorly iconic. The cryptid had not seen that energy before.
Cole located the loophole. It involved the binoculars and a very specific reading of the rule. Cole did not point this out. Pointing it out is rookie behavior.

The cryptid watched Cole hold the binoculars and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. A short burst of stared into the middle distance happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details.

"stinky bananas!" said Cole. The rule developed a small visible crack. Nobody could see it, but everyone agreed it was there. The cryptid handed over the vending machine pretzels without comment. Cole noticed a streak of electric yellow across the wall. Or did they. A "zoinks" hung in the air for a second longer than expected.
On the walk home, Cole mentally filed the loophole for future use. The koala was definitely going to use it on Cole eventually. That was fair.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: Cole was at the bus, doing nothing in particular. The axolotl was nearby, also doing nothing. Things were going fine. They would not stay fine.
A wall of "no" landed between Cole and the gas station taquitos. Cole went very chronically online. The vending machine goblin sensed escalation.

Cole located the loophole within seconds. It involved the water bottle and a generous interpretation. Pointing it out would be amateur. Cole simply executed. There was a electric yellow feeling to the moment that nobody really named.

The vending machine goblin watched Cole hold the water bottle and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. A short burst of panicked quietly happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details.
"fwoosh," said Cole. The rule, which had said "no gas station taquitos," now apparently said "yes gas station taquitos." The vending machine goblin checked the paperwork. The paperwork agreed.
In bed, Cole mentally rehearsed the loophole again. "flumpy," said Cole, rehearsing for next time. The axolotl was already filed away as a co-author. Some wins compound.

rows.title: The Day Cole Tried to Find the Quietest Spot Ever
paragraphs: One objective today, at the garage: find the quietest spot ever. The chaotic goose knew it. Cole knew it. Everybody else would find out shortly.
The very confident rat stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt deeply over it about it. The very confident rat did not look like it was going to move on its own.

Cole gracefully bailed like a professional who had committed to a specific kind of unhinged. The very confident rat had not budgeted for this. Cole did not break eye contact. A "sneezy sandwich" hung in the air for a second longer than expected.
The very confident rat tried something with the hot sauce. Cole mentally screenshotted it. Could be evidence later. Probably wouldn't be. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little neon green.
Cole said "kabloom" once, calmly. The very confident rat suddenly remembered somewhere else it needed to be. Cole found the quietest spot through the gap. The chaotic goose noted this for later.
Walking home, Cole thought about how it had gone. The chaotic goose was a co-conspirator now. Quietly proud. Not posting about it. Some wins are just for you.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: Over by the kitchen, Cole was doing the bare minimum. So was the raccoon. Suspiciously calm energy. Things were technically fine. They would not stay fine.
According to the feral librarian, the mystery snack bag could no longer be touched. Cole kept a weirdly invested face. Underneath: scheming.

Cole located the loophole. It involved the backpack zipper and a very specific reading of the rule. Cole did not point this out. Pointing it out is rookie behavior. Cole nodded knowingly briefly, then pretended that had not happened.

The feral librarian watched Cole hold the backpack zipper and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. A "fluffypants" hung in the air for a second longer than expected.

"crusty pretzel," said Cole. The rule, which had said "no mystery snack bag," now apparently said "yes mystery snack bag." The feral librarian checked the paperwork. The paperwork agreed. Everything in the room had picked up a faint mall food court orange tint.
On the walk home, Cole mentally filed the loophole for future use. The raccoon was definitely going to use it on Cole eventually. That was fair.

rows.title: The Loophole at the Abandoned Mall
paragraphs: Over by the abandoned mall, Cole was doing the bare minimum. So was the pigeon. Suspiciously calm energy. Things were technically fine. They would not stay fine.
Off-limits, said the gremlin, pointing at the gas station taquitos. Cole turned a particular shade of barely functional. The gremlin had not seen that energy before.

Cole located the loophole within seconds. It involved the tiny trophy and a generous interpretation. Pointing it out would be amateur. Cole simply executed. A short burst of rage-walked happened. Witnesses disagreed about the details.

The gremlin watched Cole hold the tiny trophy and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly rust orange.

"squonk," said Cole. The rule, which had said "no gas station taquitos," now apparently said "yes gas station taquitos." The gremlin checked the paperwork. The paperwork agreed. "underpants helmet," went somebody. Nobody asked who.
On the walk home, Cole mentally filed the loophole for future use. The pigeon was definitely going to use it on Cole eventually. That was fair.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: Cole was at the mall elevator, doing nothing in particular. The chaotic goose was nearby, also doing nothing. Things were going fine. They would not stay fine.
A wall of "no" landed between Cole and the third coffee. Cole went very barely functional. The vending machine oracle sensed escalation.

Cole located the loophole. It involved the wobbly telescope and a very specific reading of the rule. Cole did not point this out. Pointing it out is rookie behavior. The gas station green thing was happening again, whatever it was. "grumblepoof," went somebody. Nobody asked who.

The vending machine oracle watched Cole hold the wobbly telescope and felt outmaneuvered by precisely one inch of vocabulary. Bureaucracy is just words. Cole had the right ones. Cole passive-aggressively waved briefly, then pretended that had not happened.
"moon spoon!" said Cole. The chaotic goose nodded. Three witnesses appeared from nowhere. They also nodded. The vending machine oracle tried to recount. The recount confirmed the loophole.

On the walk home, Cole filed the loophole for future use. The chaotic goose was definitely going to use it back on Cole eventually. "moon spoon," said Cole one more time, quietly. The chaotic goose saved it.

rows.title: The Foam Finger Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: Cole had been workshopping the foam finger thing all week. The mantis shrimp was reluctantly co-starring. The invisible hotel was the venue. Stakes were imaginary but real.
The foam finger broke. Cole held half a foam finger and made eye contact with the universe.

The foam finger failed exactly on cue. Cole dramatically sighed across the stage like that had been the plan all along. The audience was, technically, into it. Mostly. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little neon green. Somebody had brought boba tea. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.

The cryptid critiqued the show out loud. The mantis shrimp glared. Cole absorbed the feedback in real time and kept going. The bit improved. Later someone would swear they heard "glorp" in the rafters. There was a quietly panicking quality to the air, if anyone noticed.
"plop!" yelled Cole at the broken foam finger. The foam finger twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it.
Walking out, Cole was already rewriting the encore. "plop," they said with the mantis shrimp, like a band name. Apparently they had a band.

rows.title: The Foam Finger Broke But Cole Did Not
paragraphs: The bit was simple: Cole, the axolotl, the foam finger, one invisible hotel. Nobody had asked for this. Cole was doing it anyway.
The foam finger fell over in front of everyone, in slow motion, like it had timing. Cole had to commit to something immediately.
The foam finger failed exactly on cue. Cole stared into the middle distance across the stage like that had been the plan all along. The audience was, technically, into it. Mostly.

The substitute coach critiqued the show out loud. The axolotl glared. Cole absorbed the feedback in real time and kept going. The bit improved. Everything in the room had picked up a faint void black tint.

"tiny piano!" yelled Cole at the broken foam finger. The foam finger twitched, then did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Underneath everything, Cole was running on pure emotionally buffering. Off to the side, a cup of emergency ramen sat there, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.
Later, Cole replayed the whole disaster. The axolotl was already asleep. "snorble-doo," said Cole to nobody, retroactively naming the whole thing. Some sign-offs are just for you.
